CLEAR: CLEAR SKY DETECTED AND IT IS UNACCEPTABLY CONFIDENT ABOUT ITSELF.
Wear sunglasses immediately because the sun is behaving aggressively and will attack your eyeballs without hesitation.
You may go outside and pretend to enjoy existence, but do not trust the sky because it is clearly planning something.
SUN APPARENTLY DECIDED TO SHOW UP AND RUIN ALL PLANS.
Light clothing is mandatory unless you want to melt under relentless solar judgment.
Feel free to take a walk, but the sun will glare at you with passive-aggressive intensity the whole time.
PARTLY_CLOUDY: SUN AND CLOUDS LOCKED IN AN IRRITATING POWER STRUGGLE ABOVE YOUR HEAD.
Dress in layers because the sky cannot decide whether to roast or freeze you.
Go for a casual stroll, but be ready to dodge both sudden sunlight and grumpy clouds.
THE SKY IS CONFUSED AND THINKS YOU SHOULD TOO.
Carry a jacket and sunglasses simultaneously; yes, it is that indecisive today.
Attempt outdoor activities at your own risk; expect unpredictable bursts of mood swings from the atmosphere.
CLOUDY: SUN MISSING AGAIN AND NOBODY KNOWS WHY.
Heavy clothing advised because the gray sky is passive-aggressively cold and judgmental.
Feel free to stay outside and glare at the sky, but don’t expect it to care about your discomfort.
CLOUDS CONTINUE REFUSING TO EXPLAIN THEMSELVES.
Bundle up because the atmosphere has chosen indifference as its weapon.
Consider reading indoors if you value warmth, otherwise go outside and experience the gray melancholy firsthand.
HOT: SUN APPARENTLY TRYING TO RUIN EVERYONE’S DAY.
Wear as little as legally possible to avoid spontaneous combustion.
Activities should be strictly limited to seeking shade and plotting revenge against the sun.
THE AIR ITSELF HAS JOINED THE HEAT CONSPIRACY.
Dress in breathable fabrics unless you enjoy being punished by temperatures.
Try outdoor tasks quickly, but accept that every movement is an exercise in suffering.
WINDY: Secure hats and loose clothing or risk being humiliated by physics.
Proceed outdoors only if you like running a gauntlet against invisible forces.
TREES FLAILING AROUND LIKE THEY’VE HAD ENOUGH OF HUMANITY.
Heavy jackets and scarves required to survive airborne leaf ambushes.
Consider staying inside, but if you must venture out, brace yourself for the chaos of rogue gusts.
FOGGY: VISIBILITY REDUCED TO FRUSTRATION AND REGRET.
Wear reflective clothing to prevent accidental collisions with mystery objects.
Activities outdoors are now an extreme sport; walk carefully unless you enjoy surprise encounters.
FOG HAS NO IDEA WHERE IT IS AND DOESN’T CARE.
Dress warmly but prepare for sudden wetness and general confusion.
Move slowly outside, because every step could be an existential adventure through the mist.
LIGHT_RAIN: RAIN FALLING JUST ENOUGH TO BE IRRITATING.
Wear waterproof clothing, or suffer the consequences of mild precipitation shame.
Go outside if you must, but prepare for the relentless tease of damp conditions.
CLOUDS REFUSING TO COMMIT TO ANYTHING USEFUL.
Bring a raincoat and attitude to counter the sky’s half-hearted attempts at ruin.
Casual activities outside will feel slightly sabotaged, but it’s technically still survivable.
HEAVY_RAIN: Armor yourself with full rain gear because umbrellas are now obsolete.
Outdoor activities are a disaster zone; proceed only if you enjoy heroic suffering.
CLOUDS DUMPING EVERYTHING AT ONCE OUT OF SPITE.
Waterproof clothing required, plus emotional preparation for constant dampness.
Travel outside only if you desire an impromptu swimming lesson without the pool.
THUNDER_STORM: Wear insulated clothing and prepare for shocks both literal and emotional.
Outdoor activities are officially on hold; stay inside unless seeking dramatic danger.
LIGHTNING FLASHING LIKE IT OWNS THE PLACE.
Avoid metal and dress like a cautious ninja to survive nature’s temper tantrum.
Do not attempt leisurely activities unless you want a personal electric performance.
SNOW: SNOW FALLING AGGRESSIVELY WHILE PRETENDING TO BE CALM.
Bundle up like a snow warrior or risk frostbite and humiliation.
Outdoor fun is acceptable only if you enjoy shivering and questionable transportation conditions.
COLD AIR MAKING EVERYONE MISERABLE ON PURPOSE.
Heavy coats, gloves, and boots are mandatory; fashion is irrelevant under assault.
You may venture out to shovel or stumble through snow, but know the weather is laughing at you.
